name: Can the featured snippets that show up in voice search be different from the ones I see in regular Google results?

mainEntity.name: Can the featured snippets that show up in voice search be different from the ones I see in regular Google results?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Yes, voice search can absolutely surface different featured snippets than what you see visually, especially for highly conversational or local intent queries.

Assistants often favor answers that are easiest to read aloud and most directly match natural speech.

**Key factors:**
- Voice queries are longer, more conversational, and often question-based
- Assistants may prioritize FAQ-style and Speakable-marked content
- Local “near me” and city-specific queries can shift snippet sources
- Some answers are shortened or reformatted for audio clarity

To adapt, write your key answers the way people actually speak, and include location cues where relevant (neighborhoods, landmarks, city names). Test with different devices and query styles to see which pages get read aloud.

Thinking in “spoken language first” helps you design snippets that work for both screens and voice.
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes. Voice search often picks slightly different or reformatted featured snippets that better match conversational, question-style queries and are easier to read aloud. Content structured as FAQs, using natural speech and location cues, has a better chance of being selected for voice answers.
